BareMetalMachineV1ALPHA1.description: Customer-owned bare-metal Mac allocation managed through the Compute API.

apiVersion.description: Kubernetes API version for this BareMetalMachine resource. Use `compute.mountthor.com/v1alpha1`.

metadata.description: Kubernetes object metadata for the BareMetalMachine in the tenant namespace.

spec.description: Desired customer-owned state for the BareMetalMachine.

status.description: Observed Mount Thor status for the BareMetalMachine. Customers read status; create and update requests should not set it.

failureCode.description: Customer-visible failure code when provisioning fails.

failureMessage.description: Customer-visible failure message when provisioning fails.

phase.description: Current customer-visible lifecycle phase for the BareMetalMachine.

BillingCostLineItem.description: A single itemized cost line. Quantity is in seconds; unit_price_cents is per-second from the active price book. amount_cents = quantity_seconds * unit_price_cents truncated to whole cents.

priced.description: Whether this line item has been priced.

quantity_seconds.description:
Billable seconds aggregated for this line. Closed lifecycles
have per-class minimums applied; open lifecycles report
elapsed raw time.

tenant_id.description: Stable UUID for the tenant whose usage this line attributes.

unit_price_cents.description: Per-second unit price from the customer's active price book.

usage_model.description:
`reserved` or `on-demand`, derived from the tenant's
`capacity_allocations` for this resource class.

BillingCostsPeriod.description: Closed-period or current-month window the costs response covers.

end.description: Exclusive end of the costs window.

requested.description:
`current` when computed live for the current month; otherwise
the requested `YYYY-MM` value.

start.description: Inclusive start of the costs window.

CreateCustomerApiKeyResponse.description:
Response body for creating a customer API key.

The plaintext `api_key` is returned only once and cannot be re-fetched.

api_key.description: Plaintext API key. Format: `mthr_live_*`. Returned exactly once.

api_key_id.description: Stable UUID for the API key.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

customer_user_id.description: Customer user UUID the key is bound to.

key_last4.description: Last 4 characters, used as a display affordance.

key_prefix.description:
First 18 characters of the API key, stored server-side for fast prefix
lookups.
Prefix stored for display and lookup. Not sufficient to authenticate.

plaintext_display.description: Always `"once"` — reminder that the plaintext is not retrievable.

status.description: Key lifecycle state.

tenant_id.description: Stable UUID for the tenant this key can access.

display_name.description: Optional pre-filled display name for the new user.

role_slug.description: Optional role to assign on acceptance (e.g. `mountthor-cluster-admin`, `mountthor-cluster-readonly`). When omitted, the invitee receives no role until an admin assigns one.

accept_invitation_url.description: URL for the invitee to accept the invitation.

customer_id.description: Customer the invitee will join on acceptance.

customer_user_id.description: UUID of the pending user record.

status.description:
User status after the call. Always `"invited"` for
a new invitation; an existing pending invitation returns the same.

local_port.description: Preferred local port for the customer-side VNC listener.

port_name.description: Canonical desktop port name. Must be `screen`.

ttl_seconds.description: Requested grant TTL in seconds. Omitted uses the service default.

CreateLoginRequestRequest.description: Request body for initiating a browser-based login flow.

cli_verifier_sha256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the CLI's verifier random value.

customer_slug.description: Customer identifier recorded with the registration. Format: kebab-case. When omitted, the customer is resolved after authentication completes.

localhost_redirect_uri.description:
CLI's localhost callback URI. Must match
`http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback` with a non-privileged port and no
query, fragment, or credentials.

localhost_state_sha256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the CLI's localhost-state random value.

tenant_slug.description: Optional tenant identifier to scope the eventual session to a specific tenant. When omitted the session is account-scoped.

CreateLoginRequestResponse.description: Response body for initiating a browser-based login flow.

authorization_url.description: Authorization URL the CLI opens in the customer's browser.

login_request_id.description: Stable Mount Thor identifier for this login attempt.

state_expires_in_seconds.description: Seconds until the login request expires.

CustomerAccountCapabilities.description: Capabilities and entitlements attached to the account.

entitlements.description: Entitlements currently visible to this account.

display_name.description:
Customer-owned display name. Does not change legal name, account
status, tenant binding, namespace, entitlements, or routing.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

display_name.description: Customer-owned display name shown in Mount Thor tooling.

feature_flags.description: Feature flags enabled for this account.

last_sign_in_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent completed sign-in for the principal customer admin. Null when the user has never completed a sign-in.

namespace_name.description: Kubernetes namespace that contains this tenant's compute resources.

principal_user_id.description: Customer user UUID associated with the credential used for this request.

profile_picture_url.description: URL of the user's profile picture, if available.

status.description: Account lifecycle state. `active` accounts can create keys and use compute.

tenant_id.description: Stable UUID for the default tenant returned for backwards-compatible callers.

tenant_status.description: Tenant lifecycle state. Compute calls require an active tenant.

tenants.description: Tenants in this account. Use this list to select a compute namespace.

CustomerAccountTenant.description: Tenant namespace attached to the account.

compute_api_server_url.description: Compute API server URL for this tenant.

namespace_name.description: Kubernetes namespace for this tenant's compute resources.

status.description: Tenant lifecycle state.

allowed_email_domains.description: Email domains allowed during account registration.

has_identity_integration.description:
Whether the caller's customer has any usable identity integration.
Derived from `integration_status`.

integration_status.description: Identity integration status for the caller's customer. One of `draft`, `customer-input-required`, `configured`, `testing`, `active`, `failed`, or `suspended`. `null` if no integration exists yet.

api_key_id.description: Optional client-supplied UUID for the new API key.

display_name.description: Human-readable label shown in customer tooling.

expires_at.description: Optional expiry timestamp in RFC 3339 form.

scopes.description: Allowed scopes for the new key. Defaults to `["compute"]`.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

keys.description: API-key metadata visible to the caller. Plaintext key material is never returned.

api_key_id.description: Stable UUID for the API key.

key_last4.description: Last four display characters. Not sufficient to authenticate.

key_prefix.description: Prefix stored for display and lookup. Not sufficient to authenticate.

last_used_at.description: Last successful use timestamp, if the key has been used.

revoked_at.description: Revocation timestamp when the key is no longer usable.

status.description: Key lifecycle state.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

line_items.description: Itemized line items, ordered by tenant identifier, resource kind, resource class, and usage model.

price_book_slug.description: The customer's active price book identifier at the time of the computation.

total_cents.description: Customer total = sum of `amount_cents` across all line items.

CustomerBillingInvoiceResponse.description: Customer invoice detail response (header + line items).

invoice.description: Header metadata; same shape as the list-response entry.

line_items.description:
Line items ordered by (resource_kind, resource_class,
usage_model).

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

invoices.description:
Invoices ordered by `period_start` descending then by
`created_at` descending so the most recent month is first.

total_count.description:
Total number of invoices matching the filter (independent of
`limit`/`offset`) so clients can implement pagination
indicators.

CustomerBillingPriceCatalogResponse.description:
Customer-facing price-book catalog response. Surfaces the catalog
independently of any usage rollup so creation flows (Create Bare Metal
Machine, Create VM) can render "this class costs $X/second" before the
customer commits to running anything.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

buckets.description: Customer-level time-series buckets summed across every tenant.

by_tenant.description:
Per-tenant rollup ordered by tenant identifier for stable client display.
Tenants with no usage in the window are included with empty
`by_class` and zero totals so client UIs do not need to special-case
the missing-tenant rendering.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

period.description: Window and bucket size used for this response.

summary.description: Customer-level totals summed across every tenant under the customer.

CustomerEntitlement.description: Customer-visible entitlement attached to the account.

entitlement_type.description: Entitlement category, such as `image-ref`, `machine-class`, or feature flag.

source_ref.description: Reference identifier for the entitlement grant.

CustomerFleetResponse.description: Fleet listing response for account-scoped compute resources.

bare_metal_machines.description: Bare-metal machines currently visible in the tenant namespace.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

bare_metal_machines.description: Bare-metal machines currently visible in the tenant namespace.

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace used for the tenant's compute resources.

virtual_machines.description: Virtual machines currently visible in the tenant namespace.

CustomerFleetUsageResponse.description: Fleet usage response for the requested period.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

period.description: Window and bucket size used for this response.

display_name.description: Customer display name activated by the registration.

CustomerRegistrationApiKey.description: One-time API key material returned during registration.

id.description: Stable UUID for the initial API key.

plaintext_display.description: Always `one-time`; the plaintext key cannot be fetched again.

scopes.description: Scopes granted to the initial API key.

secret.description: Plaintext API key. Format: `mthr_live_*`. Returned exactly once.

email.description: Work email address authorized by the registration invite.

name.description: Full name of the person activating the account.

CustomerRegistrationIdentitySetup.description: Identity setup link returned during registration.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 expiry timestamp for the setup URL.

status.description: Step state.

code.description: One-time registration code from Mount Thor.

contact.description: Contact who is activating the account.

account_setup_url.description: Optional account setup URL. The customer admin can visit this URL to complete account setup (set a password, sign in via social login, or use a magic link). Null when identity setup is not available for this registration.

api_key.description: Initial API key. The secret value is returned exactly once.

identity_setup.description: Optional identity setup link when SSO setup is available.

oneOf.description: Optional identity setup link when SSO setup is available.

status.description: Registration result. Always `registered` after successful activation.

namespace_name.description: Kubernetes namespace assigned to the customer's compute resources.

CustomerSessionListEntry.description:
One row of `GET /v1/admin/sessions`. Returns metadata only — never
the session token, the SHA-256 digest, or any field that would let
a reader re-use the session.

actor_type.description:
`human`, `workload`, or `api-key`. Matches the underlying actor
that minted the session.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the session was created.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the session expires.

last_used_at.description: Last successful use timestamp, if observed.

status.description: Session lifecycle state. Active sessions can authenticate compute calls.

token_fingerprint.description:
`sha256:` prefix + the first 16 hex digits of the token digest.
Safe for display; not a credential.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

sessions.description: Active sessions visible to the requesting actor.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

customer_users.description:
Customer users visible to the caller. Ordered by status
(active first, then invited, then revoked, then suspended,
then anything else) and within a status by `created_at`
ascending so the long-running members stay above newer rows.

customer_user_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer user row.

email.description: Lowercased email used as the identity binding key for this user.

status.description:
User status. Common values: `invited`, `active`,
`suspended`, `revoked`.

user_type.description:
User type. `human` for interactive users,
`api-key` for automation bindings.

FleetBareMetalMachineSummary.description: Summary of a bare-metal machine in the account fleet.

failure_code.description: Customer-visible failure code when provisioning fails.

failure_message.description: Customer-visible failure detail when provisioning fails.
failure_message.example: capacity allocation is missing for bare-metal class m4-24g

image.description: macOS image requested by the machine.

name.description: Kubernetes object name for the bare-metal machine.

phase.description: Current machine phase, such as `Pending`, `Ready`, `Failed`, or `Reclaiming`.

ready_for_access.description: Whether an access session can be opened for the machine.

FleetCapacityAllocation.description: Account capacity allocation for one resource shape.

committed_quantity.description: Reserved quantity available for the class. Null means no committed quantity.

max_active_machines.description: Maximum concurrently active bare-metal machines for this allocation.

max_allocation_duration_seconds.description: Maximum allocation duration in seconds for this allocation.

max_concurrent_access_grants.description: Maximum concurrently active access sessions for this allocation.

on_demand_allowed.description: Whether the account can burst into unreserved capacity for this class.

product.description: Product family the allocation applies to.

resource_class.description: Resource class covered by the allocation.

source_contract_line_ref.description: Optional customer contract reference for support and audit.

usage_model.description: Commercial model for the allocation, such as `reserved` or `on-demand`.

capacity_allocations.description: Capacity allocations that govern bare-metal machine and access-session limits.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

FleetBareMetalMachineSummary.description: Summary of a bare-metal machine in the account fleet.

failure_code.description: Customer-visible failure code when provisioning fails.

image.description: macOS image requested by the machine.

name.description: Kubernetes object name for the bare-metal machine.

phase.description: Current machine phase, such as `Pending`, `Ready`, `Failed`, or `Reclaiming`.

ready_for_access.description: Whether an access session can be opened for the machine.

FleetPolicyV1ALPHA1.description: Customer-visible FleetPolicy resource in the Mount Thor Compute API.

apiVersion.description: Kubernetes API version for this FleetPolicy resource. Use `compute.mountthor.com/v1alpha1`.

metadata.description: Kubernetes object metadata for the FleetPolicy in the tenant namespace.

spec.description: Desired customer-owned state for the FleetPolicy.

status.description: Observed Mount Thor status for the FleetPolicy. Customers read status; create and update requests should not set it.

failureCode.description: Customer-visible failure code when provisioning fails.

FleetResourceEntry.description: A single resource in the fleet inventory.

FleetSummaryResponse.description:
Customer-safe fleet summary including reserved capacity, spot status,
and per-resource inventory.

resources.description: Per-resource inventory with capacity source attribution.

disk_gib.description: Requested boot disk size in GiB when specified.

failure_code.description: Customer-visible failure code when provisioning fails.

failure_message.description: Customer-visible failure detail when provisioning fails.
failure_message.example: provider VM image was not available on the selected host

image_ref.description: Image reference used to boot the virtual machine.

memory_mib.description: Requested memory in MiB when specified.

name.description: Kubernetes object name for the virtual machine.

phase.description: Current VM phase, such as `Pending`, `Running`, `Failed`, or `Deleting`.

ready_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the VM became ready.

ImageV1ALPHA1.description: Customer-visible image available for bare-metal reinstall and VM workflows.

apiVersion.description: Kubernetes API version for this Image resource. Use `compute.mountthor.com/v1alpha1`.

metadata.description: Kubernetes object metadata for the Image in the tenant namespace.

spec.description: Desired customer-owned state for the Image.

status.description: Observed Mount Thor status for the Image. Customers read status; create and update requests should not set it.

failureCode.description: Customer-visible failure code when provisioning fails.

InlineTrustedIssuer.description: Inline trusted-issuer material accepted on workload-principal create so a caller can register an issuer and bind a principal in one call. The issuer is de-duplicated by canonical URL: if an active issuer already exists for the URL it is reused; otherwise it is created from this material.

allowed_clock_skew_seconds.description: Clock skew allowed when validating token time claims.

display_name.description: Customer-facing label used if a new IdP is created.

line_description.description: Optional human-readable description for the line item.

line_item_id.description: Stable UUID for the line item row.

quantity_seconds.description: Billable seconds frozen at invoice-generation time.

tenant_id.description: Tenant the line attributes usage to.

unit_price_cents.description: Per-second unit price applied to this line.

InvoiceMetadata.description:
Summary metadata for a single invoice in a list response or as
the header of a detail response.

contract_id.description:
Stable UUID for the contract this invoice was generated
against.

created_at.description: Wall-clock when the invoice row was first written.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

finalized_at.description: Wall-clock when the invoice transitioned out of `draft`.

period_end.description: Exclusive end of the billed period.

period_start.description: Inclusive start of the billed period.

status.description:
One of `open`, `paid`, `void`. Drafts are filtered before
they reach this endpoint.

intent.description: Identity setup intent. One of `sso`, `dsync`, or `domain_verification`. Defaults to `sso`.

reason.description:
Optional audit reason (8..=1000 chars when present).
When omitted, a default reason is recorded.
reason.example: customer admin clicked Setup SSO Logins

return_url.description: URL to redirect the user to after completing identity setup.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the link expires.

identity_integration_id.description: UUID of the underlying identity integration the link is scoped to.

url.description: Identity setup URL. One-time use; redirect the admin's browser to it immediately.

MachineClassV1ALPHA1.description: Customer-visible bare-metal hardware class that can be requested by BareMetalMachine resources.

apiVersion.description: Kubernetes API version for this MachineClass resource. Use `compute.mountthor.com/v1alpha1`.

metadata.description: Kubernetes object metadata for the MachineClass in the tenant namespace.

spec.description: Desired customer-owned state for the MachineClass.

MintCustomerComputeSessionRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/admin/sessions`.

Mints a short-lived Mount Thor session token from a long-lived API key.
The caller must present a valid `mthr_live_*` API key in the `Authorization`
header.

session_ttl_seconds.description:
Requested session TTL in seconds. Must be between 300 and 3600. Defaults
to 900 (15 minutes) when omitted.

source_ip.description: Optional client-reported source IP, recorded for audit.

user_agent.description: Optional client-reported user agent, recorded for audit.

MintCustomerComputeSessionResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /v1/admin/sessions`.

`session_token` is shown once; Mount Thor stores only its SHA-256 digest.

audience.description: API audience the session token is valid for.

customer_id.description: Customer account id the session is bound to.

customer_user_id.description: Customer principal id the session is bound to.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the session token expires.

issued_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the session token was issued.

namespace_name.description: Tenant namespace inside the customer's dedicated control plane.

refresh_token.description:
Optional refresh token (`mt_refresh_*`). Returned by certain grant types when refresh tokens are requested. Trade for a fresh session via the refresh-token grant
before the session expires. Stored once; cannot be re-displayed.

refresh_token_expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the refresh token expires (when set).

refresh_token_ttl_seconds.description: Refresh token TTL in seconds (when `refresh_token` is set).

session_id.description: Stable Mount Thor identifier for the minted session.

session_token.description: Short-lived bearer token. Format: `mt_session_*`. Returned exactly once.

tenant_id.description: Tenant id the session is bound to.

oneOf.description: Audit metadata for this operation, if available.

last_error.description: Present when the operation has failed. Contains the error message describing what went wrong.

PriceCatalogItem.description: An entry in the price catalog.

minimum_unit_price_cents.description: Optional minimum unit price in cents. When present, represents the floor price for this resource class and usage model.

usage_model.description:
`reserved` or `on-demand` (`hybrid` capacity is priced at the reserved rate; the catalog only emits the priced
dimensions a customer can be billed against).

credential_fingerprint.description: Non-secret fingerprint of the current credential material.

type.description: Credential type supplied for this registry.

external_id.description: External ID to require in the AWS IAM trust policy.

mount_thor_aws_principal_arn.description: Mount Thor AWS principal that must be trusted by customer AWS IAM.

required_permission_policy.description: Minimum permission policy for registry pulls.

required_scope.description: Registry host, repositories, and permissions Mount Thor needs.

oneOf.description: Registry host, repositories, and permissions Mount Thor needs.

required_trust_policy.description: Example trust policy customers can apply in AWS IAM.

type.description: Recommended credential type for this registry.

image_format.description: Image format used by the registry. Currently `mtvmbundle-v1`.

repositories.description: Repository path prefixes Mount Thor may pull from.

items.description: Repository path only. Do not include scheme, host, credentials, tag, digest, whitespace, traversal, or leading slash.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

registries.description: Registry grants visible to this tenant.

expires_at.description: Optional RFC 3339 expiry timestamp for this credential path.

external_id.description: External ID required by the role trust policy.

role_arn.description: AWS IAM role Mount Thor should assume to pull from the registry.

rotation_reason.description: Human-readable audit reason for credential rotation.

expires_at.description: Optional RFC 3339 expiry timestamp for the token.

rotation_reason.description: Human-readable audit reason for credential rotation.

token.description: Secret registry token. Accepted once and never returned.

expires_at.description: Optional RFC 3339 expiry timestamp for the password.

password.description: Secret registry password. Accepted once and never returned.

rotation_reason.description: Human-readable audit reason for credential rotation.

auth.description: Replacement credential material. Secret values are accepted once and never returned.

registry_host.description: Registry host Mount Thor needs to access.

repositories.description: Repository path prefixes Mount Thor needs to access.

auth.description: Credential metadata. Secret material is never returned.

oneOf.description: Credential metadata. Secret material is never returned.

auth_requirements.description: Setup requirements when credentials have not been supplied yet.

oneOf.description: Setup requirements when credentials have not been supplied yet.

image_format.description: Image format used by this grant.

repositories.description: Repository path prefixes Mount Thor may pull from.

status.description: Grant lifecycle state.

RegistryPullReference.description: Opaque runtime pull reference for a registry connection.

name.description: Opaque pull reference name. This is not a credential.

RevokeCustomerApiKeyResponse.description: Response body for revoking a customer API key.

active_sessions_revoked.description:
Count of active sessions tied to this key that were revoked at the
same time.

api_key_id.description: Stable UUID for the revoked API key.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

customer_user_id.description: Customer user UUID the key was bound to.

key_last4.description: Last four display characters. Not sufficient to authenticate.

key_prefix.description: Prefix stored for display and lookup. Not sufficient to authenticate.

revoked_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the key was revoked.

revoked_by.description: Actor reference that revoked the key.

status.description: Always `"revoked"` after a successful revoke.

in_use.description: Spot bare-metal slots currently in use.

enabled.description: Whether spot capacity is enabled for this tenant.

monthly_spend_limit_cents.description: Monthly spend limit in cents. Zero when spot is disabled.

used_this_month_cents.description: Spot spend consumed this billing month in cents.

disk_gib_in_use.description: Spot disk in GiB currently in use.

memory_gib_in_use.description: Spot memory in GiB currently in use.

TenantUsageBreakdown.description:
Per-tenant usage rollup nested inside the customer response so
customers with multiple tenants can attribute aggregate spend.

billable_seconds.description: Billable seconds for this tenant after per-class minimums.

by_class.description: Tenant usage grouped by resource class.

lifecycle_count.description: Number of closed or open lifecycles for this tenant in the window.

raw_active_seconds.description: Wall-clock active seconds for this tenant before minimums.

audiences.description:
Accepted `aud` values. At least one is required: an empty list would
make audience validation inert at token exchange.

issuer.description:
OIDC issuer identifier. Stored canonicalized (trailing slash stripped)
so the token `iss` claim is compared against a single normalized form.

jwks_document_ref.description:
Out-of-band JWKS document reference. Required when `jwks_source_type`
is `stored-jwks`.

jwks_uri.description: Explicit JWKS URI. Required when `jwks_source_type` is `https`.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

allowed_clock_skew_seconds.description: Clock skew allowed when validating token time claims.

issuer.description: Canonical OIDC issuer URL used to match the token `iss` claim.

status.description: IdP lifecycle state. Disabled IdPs cannot mint sessions.

TrustedIssuerPatchRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /v1/admin/identity/providers/{id}`.

The trust root (issuer URL + JWKS source) is intentionally immutable —
re-register to rotate it, so a silent change of which keys are trusted
cannot ride in on an update. Mutable: label, accepted audiences, clock
skew, and `status` (disable / re-enable).

allowed_clock_skew_seconds.description: Replacement clock skew allowance in seconds.

status.description: New lifecycle state: `active` or `disabled`.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

UsageBucket.description: Usage measurement for one resource group in a period.

by_class.description: Usage grouped by resource class inside this bucket.

UsageByClass.description: Usage measurement grouped by resource class.

billable_seconds.description: Billable seconds after per-class minimums are applied to closed lifecycles.

lifecycle_count.description: Number of closed or open lifecycles counted in this group.

raw_active_seconds.description: Wall-clock active seconds before billing minimums are applied.

resource_class.description: Resource class or image class that usage is grouped under.

resource_kind.description: Compute resource family, such as `BareMetalMachine` or `VirtualMachine`.

end.description: Exclusive end of the usage window.

start.description: Inclusive start of the usage window.

UsageSummary.description: Aggregate usage totals for the requested period.

billable_seconds.description: Total billable seconds across the requested window.

by_class.description: Window totals grouped by resource class.

lifecycle_count.description: Total lifecycle count across the requested window.

raw_active_seconds.description: Total wall-clock active seconds across the requested window.

VirtualMachineV1ALPHA1.description: Customer-visible VirtualMachine resource in the Mount Thor Compute API.

apiVersion.description: API version for this resource. Use `compute.mountthor.com/v1alpha1`.

metadata.description: Kubernetes object metadata for the VirtualMachine in the tenant namespace.

spec.description: VirtualMachineSpec defines the desired state of a VirtualMachine.

items.description: VMPort specifies a port to expose on a virtual machine.

resources.description: VMResources specifies the resource requirements for a virtual machine.

status.description: VirtualMachineStatus defines the observed state of a VirtualMachine.

items.description: Condition contains details for one aspect of the current state of this API Resource.

lastTransitionTime.description:
lastTransitionTime is the last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.
This should be when the underlying condition changed.  If that is not known, then using the time when the API field changed is acceptable.

message.description:
message is a human readable message indicating details about the transition.
This may be an empty string.

observedGeneration.description:
observedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon.
For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions[x].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date
with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason.description:
reason contains a programmatic identifier indicating the reason for the condition's last transition.
Producers of specific condition types may define expected values and meanings for this field,
and whether the values are considered a guaranteed API.
The value should be a CamelCase string.
This field may not be empty.

status.description: status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

type.description: type of condition in CamelCase or in foo.example.com/CamelCase.

failureCode.description: Customer-visible failure code when provisioning fails.

failureMessage.description: Customer-visible failure message when provisioning fails.

phase.description: Current customer-visible lifecycle phase for the VirtualMachine.

items.description: VMPublishedPort describes a customer-safe private access command for a VM port.

WorkloadPrincipalCreateRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/admin/identity/principals`.

Exactly one of `trusted_issuer_id` (reference an already-registered issuer)
or `issuer` (inline material, de-duplicated into the issuer registry) is
required.

claim_constraints.description:
Claim constraints (required + non-empty when `subject_match_type` is
`claim-constraints`; must be empty for `exact`).

issuer.description: Inline IdP material. Mutually exclusive with trusted_issuer_id.

oneOf.description: Inline issuer material. Mutually exclusive with `trusted_issuer_id`.

max_session_ttl_seconds.description: Maximum TTL (seconds) for sessions minted via this binding [300, 3600].

scopes.description:
Scopes the minted session carries. Empty yields a session restricted to
the issuer/tenant defaults.

subject_match_value.description:
The required `sub` (for `exact`) or a descriptor label (for
`claim-constraints`). Always non-empty; participates in the binding's
uniqueness key.

tenant_id.description: Tenant the minted sessions are bound to. Must belong to the customer.

trusted_issuer_id.description: Reference to an existing trusted issuer. Mutually exclusive with `issuer`.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

principals.description: Workload principals registered for this customer.

claim_constraints.description: Additional claim constraints used when `subject_match_type` is `claim-constraints`.

max_session_ttl_seconds.description: Maximum session TTL in seconds for this principal.

scopes.description: Scopes granted to sessions minted by this principal.

status.description: Principal lifecycle state. Disabled principals cannot mint sessions.

subject_match_value.description: Required subject value or descriptor for the claim constraint.

tenant_id.description: Stable tenant UUID this principal can mint sessions into.

trusted_issuer_id.description: Stable IdP UUID this principal trusts.

max_session_ttl_seconds.description: Replacement maximum session TTL in seconds.

scopes.description: Replacement scopes granted to newly minted sessions.

status.description: New lifecycle state: `active` or `disabled`.

customer_id.description: Stable UUID for the customer account.

201.description: Desktop grant created or existing active grant returned

403.description: Access denied

409.description: Resource not ready or grant revoked

201.description: SSH grant created or existing active grant returned

403.description: Access denied

409.description: Resource not ready or grant revoked

201.description: Tunnel grant created or existing active grant returned

403.description: Access denied

409.description: Resource not ready or grant revoked

parameters.description: Grant identifier to revoke
parameters.name: grant_id

403.description: Access denied

get.description:
Returns account identity, tenant namespace mapping, and customer-visible
entitlements for the bearer API key. Use `tenants[].namespace_name` when
configuring `kubectl` for the Compute API.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing account:read scope

patch.description:
This operation only updates customer-owned display metadata. It does not
change legal name, tenant bindings, namespaces, entitlements, routing, or
account status.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing account:write scope

get.description:
Joins live usage against the customer's active price book and
returns itemized line items per (tenant, resource_kind,
resource_class, usage_model). For `period=current` the math is
recomputed on every request; for past months the response
reflects the frozen invoice when available.

parameters.description: `current` or a closed calendar month `YYYY-MM`.
parameters.name: period

403.description: Credential is missing billing:read scope, or your account does not have billing access.

409.description: Customer has no active contract; cannot derive a price book

get.description:
Returns paginated invoice headers ordered by `period_start`
descending. Drafts are filtered. Optional filters on `status`
and `period_after` allow clients to scope to recent history or
to a specific lifecycle state (e.g. only `open` for billing
dashboards, only `void` for audit views).

parameters.description: Optional status filter: `open`, `paid`, or `void`.
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Optional inclusive lower bound on `period_start` (YYYY-MM-DD).
parameters.name: period_after

parameters.description: Page size, clamped to [1, 200]; default 50.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Result offset for pagination; default 0.
parameters.name: offset

400.description: Invalid status filter or period_after format

403.description: Credential is missing billing:read scope, or your account does not have billing access.

get.description:
The invoice must belong to the authenticated customer; a
not-found shape is returned even when the invoice exists for a
different customer so this endpoint cannot be used to probe for
invoice IDs across tenants.

parameters.description: Invoice UUID.
parameters.name: invoice_id

403.description: Credential is missing billing:read scope, or your account does not have billing access.

404.description: Invoice not found, or does not belong to the authenticated customer

get.description:
Returns the active price book identifier + every priced
(resource_kind, resource_class, usage_model) triple, ordered by
that key. Independent of usage —
surfaces what the customer *would* be billed if they ran a given
class, so machine or VM creation flows can display a
per-class price hint before submission.

403.description: Credential is missing billing:read scope, or your account does not have billing access.

409.description: Customer has no active contract or the active contract has no price book assigned

get.description:
Returns usage aggregated across every tenant under the customer
account, plus a per-tenant breakdown. Window semantics match
`/v1/admin/fleet/usage`: `24h` -> hourly buckets, `7d` and
`month` -> daily buckets. Open lifecycles report elapsed raw time;
per-class billing minimums apply only on closed lifecycles.

parameters.description: Window: `24h`, `7d`, or `month`.
parameters.name: period

403.description: Credential is missing billing:read scope, or your account does not have billing access.

get.description: List customer users. Returns user metadata including email, display name, status, and timestamps.

403.description: Credential is missing users:read scope
Credential is missing users:write scope

409.description: An active customer user already exists with this email, or the customer's identity integration is not yet configured

503.description: Identity service unavailable or rejected the invitation.

get.description:
Returns a customer-safe snapshot of the tenant namespace across bare-metal
machines and virtual machines. Only customer-relevant fields are included
in this projection.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing compute:read scope

get.description:
Returns commercial and operational limits that apply to the tenant's fleet:
committed quantities, whether on-demand use is allowed, bare-metal machine
caps, and access-session caps.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing compute:read scope

get.description:
Returns a customer-safe aggregate view of reserved capacity, spot
billing state, and per-resource inventory. Uses "reserved" terminology
(not "committed") in all customer-facing fields.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing compute:read scope

get.description:
Returns usage grouped by resource class for one of three windows: `24h`,
`7d`, or `month`. Open lifecycles report elapsed raw time; billing minimums
are applied only when the lifecycle closes.

parameters.description: Window: `24h`, `7d`, or `month`.
parameters.name: period

200.description: Fleet usage view for the tenant

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing compute:read scope

get.description: Get identity configuration for this account, including integration status and allowed domains.

403.description: Credential is missing account:read scope

post.description: Generate a one-time link for the account admin to configure SSO, add a domain, or set up Directory Sync. The returned URL expires shortly after creation.

403.description: Credential is missing users:write scope

404.description: No eligible identity integration for this customer

get.description:
Returns OIDC workload bindings that can exchange external tokens for short
lived Mount Thor sessions.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing principals:read scope

post.description:
Creates a binding from a verified external OIDC token to Mount Thor session
scopes. The request must reference an existing IdP or provide inline IdP
material that Mount Thor can register or reuse.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing principals:write scope

404.description: Referenced tenant or trusted issuer does not exist for this customer

409.description: A principal already exists for this (issuer, subject) binding

patch.description:
Updates mutable principal metadata, scopes, TTL, matching constraints, and
lifecycle status. Disabling a principal blocks new token exchanges; already
minted sessions keep their own expiry.

parameters.description: Principal UUID
parameters.name: id

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing principals:write scope

404.description: Principal does not exist for this customer

get.description:
Returns external OIDC providers trusted by this customer. These records
contain trust configuration only; they do not contain token or key material.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing issuers:read scope

post.description:
Adds an external OIDC issuer to the customer's trust boundary. The issuer URL
is canonicalized on write, and active issuers must be unique per customer.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing issuers:write scope

409.description: An active issuer already exists for this issuer URL

patch.description:
Updates mutable IdP metadata: display name, accepted audiences, clock skew,
and status. The issuer URL and JWKS source are immutable; register a new IdP
to change those trust roots.

parameters.description: IdP UUID
parameters.name: id

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing issuers:write scope

404.description: IdP does not exist for this customer

409.description: Re-enabling collides with another active IdP for this URL

get.description:
Plaintext API keys and verifier material are never returned.
Revoked keys remain visible for a period after revocation.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing keys:read scope

post.description:
The plaintext `api_key` is returned exactly once. The requested scopes must
be covered by the caller's own API-key scopes.
Store the returned value in a secret manager before discarding the response.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing keys:write scope

delete.description:
Revocation is immediate for new authentication attempts. Existing
short-lived sessions minted from the key expire on their own schedule.

parameters.description: API key UUID to revoke
parameters.name: id

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing keys:write scope

404.description: API key does not exist for this customer

parameters.description: Page size, clamped to [1, 200]. Default 50.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Offset from the start. Default 0.
parameters.name: offset

403.description: Credential is missing account:read scope

post.description:
Redeems a one-time registration code, activates the account, and returns the
initial API key exactly once. The route accepts JSON and the hosted HTML
registration form.

401.description: Registration code is invalid, expired, or revoked

get.description:
Returns registry metadata and setup status only. Secret credential material
and pull secrets are never returned.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing registries:read scope

post.description:
Creates the registry metadata and returns any provider-specific setup
requirements. Supply credentials later with `PATCH /v1/admin/registries/{id}`.

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing registries:write scope

delete.description:
Disables future use of the registry grant for image pulls. Existing compute
resources continue according to their own lifecycle.

parameters.description: Registry grant id
parameters.name: id

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing registries:write scope

404.description: Registry grant does not exist for this tenant

get.description:
Returns metadata and credential fingerprints for one registry grant. Secret
material is never returned.

parameters.description: Registry grant id
parameters.name: id

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing registries:read scope

404.description: Registry grant does not exist for this tenant

patch.description:
Accepts new credential material for a registry grant. The server validates
shape, stores only protected credential material and fingerprints, and never
returns the submitted secret.

parameters.description: Registry grant id
parameters.name: id

401.description: Missing or invalid customer API key

403.description: API key is missing registries:write scope

404.description: Registry grant does not exist for this tenant

get.description: Lists active session metadata for the requesting actor. Token material is never returned, and sessions outside the actor scope are not disclosed.

200.description: Active sessions for the requesting actor

403.description: Credential is missing account:read scope

post.description:
The returned `session_token` is the bearer credential used for the Compute
API. It is returned once, expires automatically, and cannot be renewed; mint
a replacement session when it expires.

403.description: API key revoked, expired, or out of scope

post.description: Create a login request. Returns the authorization URL the CLI opens in the browser. The flow completes at `POST /v1/admin/sessions` with `grant_type=login-handoff`.

404.description: Identity federation disabled, or customer / identity integration not found

delete.description: Revokes one active session by id within the requesting actor scope. Returns 404 when the session is absent or belongs to another actor.

parameters.description: Session UUID
parameters.name: id

403.description: Credential is missing account:write scope

404.description: Session does not exist for this actor
